danger_signals.visual_form: Player directed off-ice to penalty box area
danger_signals.meaning: Rule violation occurred; team temporarily disadvantaged (2-4 min depending on injury severity)

danger_signals.visual_form: Two players drop gloves and engage in physical combat animation
danger_signals.meaning: Heated conflict; 5-minute major penalty consequences incoming

danger_signals.visual_form: Performance quality decline over time during play
danger_signals.meaning: Player stamina depleted; offensive/defensive effectiveness reduced until line rotation or timeout called

danger_signals.visual_form: Restricted alternate line access during power play
danger_signals.meaning: Numerical disadvantage; team operating with fewer substitution options

safety_signals.visual_form: Manual line cycling before face-off or automatic substitution
safety_signals.meaning: Stamina refreshed; player performance quality restored to optimal

safety_signals.visual_form: Game pause with timeout option selection
safety_signals.meaning: Strategic rest period; team stamina reset (limited to once per game)

safety_signals.visual_form: Six-tone audio signal marking end of penalty duration
safety_signals.meaning: Infraction served; player returns to ice with full roster capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Manual or auto goalie mode toggle and puck save state
safety_signals.meaning: Defensive security; player maintains active control over goal protection

faction_indicators.color_identity: Team-specific color schemes (e.g., Edmonton Oilers visual identity)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opponent team roster displays with distinct uniform coloration
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Team-affiliated scoreboard displays, penalty box assignment by team affiliation
Penalty call animations, face-off positioning, rule enforcement visualization

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Scoreboard display showing numerical advantage; opponent penalty box occupied
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Restricted alternate lines for defending team; offensive player mobility increased

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player performance quality visibly degraded during extended ice time
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced speed, accuracy, and defensive positioning effectiveness

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player directed to penalty box; scoreboard penalty indicator updated
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Removal from active play for 2-4 minute duration (adjustable by injury state)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Scoreboard display with menu options; game clock stopped
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Instant replay review accessible; line editing, goalie substitution, game abort options available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Combat animation between two players; physical interaction rendered
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Both players receive 5-minute major penalties upon fight resolution

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Goalie puck possession animation; player control assumption
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Clear/pass/flip options available; face-off drawable through input avoidance

environmental_storytelling.example: Scoreboard displays ongoing game information, penalty status, period breaks, instant replays—all communicating current world state without explicit text narration
Real-life 1994-95 NHL season rosters embed historical context; player names and positions authenticate team identity
Physical removal of penalized player to penalty box area; visual isolation communicates temporary roster disadvantage
Referee intervention (whistle, signal, face-off positioning) visualizes rule enforcement and conflict resolution
Fatigue mechanic rendered through observable player behavior decline; no stat overlay required—player controls reveal stamina state

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fluid stick-handling, directional puck movement, dump/pass/shot execution, spin-o-rama execution at full capability
Reduced movement speed, degraded shot accuracy, limited agility in stick-handling
Hold, hook, poke check, body check, shot-blocking available at normal responsiveness
Immobilized off-ice; roster unavailable until six-tone audio signal marks penalty expiration
Punch throws to head, uppercuts, jersey grab/pull, skating back-forth, punch block attempts
Dive, poke check, save attempt execution; post-save transitions to puck management (clear/pass/flip/face-off)
Automated save attempts; player assumes control only upon puck save
Whistle animation, penalty signal, player direction to penalty box, face-off positioning
Reduced tactical execution, predictable positioning, lower skill check success rate
Enhanced tactical execution, improved positioning prediction, higher skill check success rate

notes: NHL '96 (Genesis) communicates world-state primarily through real-time behavioral manifestation and spatial positioning rather than explicit UI overlays. Danger is signaled through penalty mechanics (box assignment, fight initiation) and fatigue degradation. Safety is reinforced through stamina management options (line rotation, timeout). Faction identity derives from roster authenticity and team-specific visual branding rather than narrative faction systems. The game's 'Virtual Stadium' technology (DOS version, referenced in source) establishes visual precedent for the Genesis iteration's graphics fidelity. Political/hierarchical dynamics exist implicitly through referee authority enforcement and power-play numerical advantage mechanics.
